summary: Realtime Firestore listener hook that subscribes to vendors/{vendorID} and exposes the onboarding completion state for the carrier admin wizard. Returns the onboarding object (isComplete, steps, completedAt) and a markStepComplete() helper that writes back to the same document.

businessIntent.problem: The wizard needs to know which steps have been completed and whether onboarding is fully done, so it can show per-tab progress indicators and transition from setup mode to management mode.
businessIntent.expectedOutcome: Hook subscribes to vendors/{vendorID} and returns { onboarding, loading, error }. markStepComplete(stepKey) writes vendors/{vendorID} with updateDoc, marking the step boolean as true.

selectedBuildingBlocks.name: realtime-firestore-listener
selectedBuildingBlocks.reason: Subscribes to vendors/{vendorID} doc via onSnapshot to reflect step completion in realtime

selectedBuildingBlocks.name: hook-service-pattern
selectedBuildingBlocks.reason: Standard { data, loading, error } hook contract and markStepComplete async helper

selectedBuildingBlocks.name: loading-empty-error-state
selectedBuildingBlocks.reason: Must handle loading, missing onboarding field (default to empty state), and Firestore errors

existingPatternsToFollow: doc(db, 'vendors', vendorID) for the document path
onSnapshot on a single document ref — return unsubscribe from useEffect
Extract the onboarding field from doc.data() — default to null if missing
markStepComplete uses updateDoc with dot-notation field path: 'onboarding.steps.{stepKey}': true
knownAntiPatternsToAvoid: Do not use a subcollection — onboarding is a field inside vendors/{vendorID}, not a separate collection
Do not forget to return unsubscribe from useEffect
markStepComplete must use updateDoc (merge), not setDoc (overwrite)

create.description: Realtime onSnapshot hook for vendors/{vendorID}.onboarding field. Returns { onboarding, loading, error } and markStepComplete(stepKey) helper that writes back with updateDoc.
Jest tests: loading:true initially, onboarding returned when field exists, null when field missing, unsubscribe on unmount, markStepComplete calls updateDoc with correct dot-notation path.

hookSpec.returnShape: { onboarding: { isComplete: boolean, steps: Record<string, boolean>, completedAt: any } | null, loading: boolean, error: Error | null, markStepComplete: (stepKey: string) => Promise<void> }

dataModelsTouched.notes: Reads vendors/{vendorID}.onboarding via onSnapshot. markStepComplete writes with updateDoc using dot-notation: 'onboarding.steps.{stepKey}': true. Schema: { isComplete: boolean, steps: { dispatch, drivers, trucks, trailers, locations, contact }, completedAt: Timestamp|null }

failureEscalationRules: If updateDoc mock fails: reload realtime-firestore-listener building block and verify mock ordering
If onSnapshot mock fails: reload testing-guide building block and regenerate test

steps: Produce handoff package for Claude Code integration

claudeCodeIntegrationPlan.reason: Factory output is an integration candidate only — Claude Code copies files into production repo and validates.

acceptanceCriteria: Hook subscribes to vendors/{vendorID} via onSnapshot
Returns { onboarding, loading, error } — onboarding is null when field is absent
loading is true on initial render, false after first snapshot
markStepComplete(stepKey) calls updateDoc with dot-notation path 'onboarding.steps.{stepKey}': true
